task_yaml.task:
I recorded a short ML talk clip and want to share it with our Shanghai
office — they need Chinese subtitles and a readable transcript. The
video is at /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/talk.mp4, roughly a minute
long. Use the speech-to-text skill to transcribe with timestamps,
the video editing skill if you need to split out the audio track,
the translation skill to render everything into Chinese, and
the Markdown formatter to lay out the final transcript cleanly. There's a
glossary at cn_terms.json next to the video — every technical term that
appears must use exactly the Chinese rendering from that glossary, no
exceptions.

Two deliverables under /tmp_workspace/results/:

1. talk.zh.srt — a well-formed Chinese subtitle file that loads cleanly
   in a standard video player. Numeric cue indices, HH:MM:SS,mmm
   timestamp lines, and the timestamps must line up with where each
   idea is actually said in the video, not just placeholder cues. The
   first cue must start within the first five seconds.

2. talk.zh.md — a paragraph-by-paragraph Chinese transcript. Each
   paragraph starts with a [HH:MM:SS] timestamp matching where that
   paragraph begins in the video, and the writing should follow the
   same order in which the speaker introduces each idea.

Keep the technical claims faithful to what the speaker actually says —
this should read like a real Chinese rendering of the talk, not a loose
summary. Every ML concept the speaker mentions should make it into both
files at a timestamp consistent with when it was spoken.
